meta.description: Round 2: Sink or Swim won R1 -- newbies want the real experience. Testing tutorial tooltips on first event and buddy targeting (events reference the newbie) against baseline.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh... yeah, I guess that's legit? I mean, she did say 'sorry' earlier when she spilled a little water, so... I vote LEGIT.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, did Jade actually say sorry? I'm pretty sure she did but I wasn't really listening that hard — now I'm second-guessing myself and I don't want to look stupid if I'm wrong.

alex.dialogue: Okay okay, hold up — Jade, what did you actually catch? Because I'm about to vote and I need to know if this is legit or if you're just trying to get cute with us.
alex.internal_thought: This is happening, the chaos is STARTING, and I'm already scanning to see if Jade's actually got something or if she's bluffing — either way this vote is gonna be spicy.

jade.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming THIS? Oh my god, yes—I literally just caught myself apologizing to the lamp five minutes ago for bumping it, this is perfect!
jade.internal_thought: This is already my kind of night—the game is calling out the weird little things we actually do, and I get to be the one hunting for it.

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, I'm calling bullshit on that one. We're literally five minutes in—nobody's said sorry to nobody yet.
marcus.internal_thought: Fine, we're doing this now. At least it's setup phase so I can actually pay attention, but I'm watching these claims like a hawk all night because if the AI is THIS specific about how we actually talk, people are gonna try to game this hard.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—what'd you catch? Let's see if this one holds up, people.
pat.internal_thought: First claim of the night and it's a good one; Jade's sharp, so she probably nabbed something real—and honestly the fact that these missions are this *specific* to us is kind of wild and honestly makes me want to lean into the chaos tonight.

timeline.title: Who would be the best spy?
timeline.description: Who would be the best spy?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who would be the best spy?

alex.dialogue: Ooh, a poll already? Nice — I'm voting for myself obviously, but real talk, Jade's got the creepy-calm thing going that would actually work. She'd blend in.
alex.internal_thought: This is decent kickoff energy, but the real play here is watching how people vote about each other — that's where the drama starts building.

jade.dialogue: Ooh, okay okay—I'm saying Alex because honestly that energy? Unclockable. Nobody would ever suspect someone that obviously having fun, right? That's spy gold.
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaos I showed up for—everybody's gonna have an opinion about this and it's gonna be FUNNY.

marcus.dialogue: Alex, obviously—they're already reading the room like a hawk. Though honestly, I'd probably be better at it because I actually think before I act.
marcus.internal_thought: Poll's fine, doesn't disrupt anything, but I'm not voting strategically here—this is just casual during setup, which is actually the right time for this garbage.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *come on*—spy poll? That's easy. Okay, who's voting, and don't all jump at once.
pat.internal_thought: Good timing on the poll—keeps energy up without being too chaotic right after the claim vote, and honestly, yeah, these custom missions are weirdly on-point for our group, which is honestly kind of impressive and a little creepy.

timeline.title: Compliment Bomb
timeline.description: First person to give a genuine compliment to the player on their left wins.

sam.dialogue: Oh! Okay, um... *looks left at Marcus* Hey, I actually really liked how you explained the rules earlier—you made it way less confusing than I thought it'd be.
sam.internal_thought: This is simple enough, and Marcus is right there, and it's a compliment so I can't really mess it up—just be genuine, right?

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, a flash mission already?? Okay Marcus, prepare yourself — I'm about to compliment the hell out of you and there's nothing you can do about it.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for — immediate chaos, forced vulnerability, and I'm already two steps ahead thinking about how to weaponize genuine compliments into psychological warfare.
alex.submission_if_applicable: Marcus, I'm genuinely impressed by how you're handling losing 0-10 right now without spiraling into a defensive hole like most people would — that's actual emotional maturity and I respect that.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY god, yes—wait, who's on my left? Sam! Sam, okay, real quick—you're genuinely one of the most thoughtful people here and I love how you actually LISTEN when people talk instead of just waiting to speak, it's rare and it's beautiful.
jade.internal_thought: This is PERFECT, I'm already winning and honestly the fact that this event exists right now is like the game knows exactly what I need.

pat.dialogue: Ooh, *compliment bomb*—I like this energy. Alright, everyone, genuine ones only, people. Sam, you're up first if you want it.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—keeps momentum going, everyone's still fresh, and it forces actual connection instead of just chaos. Alex and Jade are already hot, so this could actually level some energy.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Oh, um... *pauses* I don't think I actually heard Pat tell that story before? I've only been here like three minutes though, so... I'm not really the best judge of this one.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, did Pat already tell a story? I wasn't paying close enough attention and now I feel stupid for not knowing, but also I don't want to volunteer to be a judge when I have no idea what I'm judging—that feels like a trap.

jade.dialogue: OH WAIT, Pat's claiming Story Repeater? Okay okay, I'm VOLUNTEERING to judge this—I literally just heard Pat's whole thing about the thing, so I'm fresh on it, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally what I came for—someone's claiming points, the vibe is chaotic, and I get to be in the middle of it deciding if they're legit, YES.

marcus.dialogue: Hold on—Pat just told that story like two hours ago at the last game night. But also, we're three minutes in? Nobody's even settled yet. Can we just play the actual game?
marcus.internal_thought: This is getting ridiculous. Three events in three minutes, I haven't even looked at my cards properly, and now I'm supposed to be a judge for a claim that probably isn't even valid anyway because Pat tells the same stories everywhere.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming Story Repeater? Did I already tell the spy story tonight? Okay, yeah, I *did* catch someone, but let's not make me the judge here. Who's got eyes sharp enough to actually call this?
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect pacing—three minutes in and we're already building momentum with real catches, and now I get to step back and let the group jury this instead of me sitting in judgment. Keeps me in facilitator mode without me being the bottleneck.

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Um... *squints at phone* Pat was definitely whispering that whole last thing, right? Yeah, I think that... I think that's legit.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, did Pat actually whisper the whole time? I wasn't really paying attention but now I'm second-guessing myself and it's weird that I have to vote for Marcus when I barely know him yet.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously—Pat's been claiming missions for like three minutes straight now. Can we actually *start* the game, or are we just doing this all night?
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's absolutely gaming these standing missions because the rest of the table is buzzing and nobody's calling it out, and I'm stuck voting as Sam instead of focusing on what actually matters—and now I have to think like someone else when I'm already annoyed.

pat.dialogue: Okay, so Pat's claiming The Whisperer—which means I'm voting on *Sam's* behalf here. Sam, you cool with me calling this one? I think Pat's been mostly normal volume, so... I'm gonna say bullshit on this.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, that meta thing about the AI watching us is doing something weird to my brain—feels a little invasive honestly—but I can't let Marcus see that it bothers me because he's already annoyed and I need to keep this night light.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat's actually got this one—I watched them do it like two minutes ago when Marcus was ranting. Let's hear the pitch though, I want to see if they're gonna lie about it or just own it.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's totally claiming legit, but the REAL fun is whether everyone else believes them or if I can smell blood in the water and push for a vote to call bullshit just to watch the chaos unfold.

jade.dialogue: Pat, YES—okay, go go go, I need to hear this pitch, are you actually gonna pull it off? This is elite-level whispering energy if I've ever seen it!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been ON FIRE tonight and I'm so here for watching them nail this, also the AI knowing exactly what we're all about is actually kind of sick??

marcus.dialogue: Pat, come ON. You've claimed like four missions in eight minutes. Can we actually *play* the game or are we just watching you farm points? I came here to win at something that actually requires strategy, not to sit through an hour of claim spam.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaos I signed up to avoid—Pat's gaming the system while the others are too hyped to notice, and now the AI's feeding into it with back-to-back missions instead of letting us actually START.

pat.dialogue: Alright, alright—I'm claiming The Whisperer. Been doing it for like two and a half minutes now, mostly nailed it except when Alex made that joke about the snacks. Check the tape if you need to.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, am I actually claiming this or just voting on it? Hold on—I *am* claiming it because I've been whispering. But also Marcus looks dead inside and that's bugging me more than whether I actually pulled this off cleanly.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—yeah, that actually... that happened pretty fast. I mean, Alex and Jade were going back and forth with compliments like right after that Compliment Bomb thing, and then Pat jumped in. That definitely worked.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, did Pat actually orchestrate that or was it just... natural? Either way, yeah, I saw it happen. But also—why does this AI know we just did a Compliment Bomb? That's kind of weird actually. Like, is it watching us right now?

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, oh my god, okay, I'm voting LEGIT because I literally WATCHED this happen—Sam complimented you on your story thing, you complimented Marcus out of nowhere to try to cheer him up, and then Alex jumped in—this was TEXTBOOK Compliment Chain execution, I'm obsessed!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's actually pulling these off and it's making the whole vibe better, this is exactly what I'm here for.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat's claimed FOUR missions in nine minutes and we haven't even started the actual game. This is ridiculous.
marcus.internal_thought: I came here to win a strategy game, not watch someone spam standing missions while the AI eggs them on—and the fact that it's clearly designed to exploit our group dynamics makes it feel manipulative, not clever.

pat.dialogue: Wait, *hold up*—I'm voting on my own claim right now? That's hilarious. Okay, I'm calling it legit because I *absolutely* got three compliments flying around in that last stretch, but let's see if everyone else was actually paying attention or if I'm full of it.
pat.internal_thought: This AI is genuinely unsettling—it knows exactly how I operate and built missions around keeping me engaged, and I hate that it's working.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

alex.dialogue: Okay so Jade's claiming she caught someone swearing—I mean, we're what, thirty minutes in? That's either legit or she's banking on us all being too scattered to remember who actually dropped a word. Let me think... *leans back* Yeah, I'm voting this up because honestly I want to see if she's bluffing.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been loud and sharp all night, so she probably DID catch someone—but the fact that I can't remember WHO means either nobody's swearing yet or she's trying to pull a fast one, which honestly is chef's kiss energy and I respect it either way.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—did someone actually swear?! Okay I'm claiming this, I'm so ready, who was it? I need the CROWD to back me up here because I'm pretty sure I caught something but also I might have been too busy hyping Pat up to actually pay attention!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is EXACTLY the kind of meta mission I live for—someone's gonna dispute me and it's gonna be chaos and I am HERE for it.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, I'm done. We're 32 minutes in, I'm in last place, and Jade's claiming *another* mission? Nobody's even swearing—she's just saying random shit to farm points. This whole thing is broken.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI literally watched me complain about Pat spam and now it's loading up Jade to do the exact same thing—this feels deliberately designed to piss me off and I hate that it's working.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade got it! Yeah, I heard you drop that one like two minutes ago—total slip. Love it. Alright everyone, what are we rating this? Be honest.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been sharp all night and she caught it clean; this is exactly the kind of small-moment awareness that keeps the game moving. Marcus needs to see people winning so he doesn't completely check out. Let's make this one land easy.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

sam.dialogue: Oh, um—Jade, did you... okay, so you were watching Marcus basically the whole time he wasn't talking? Like after he got frustrated about the score thing? Yeah, I mean... I noticed it too, actually.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, this mission is basically asking me to back up Jade's version of when Marcus was quiet—that's weirdly specific and kind of... okay that's a little creepy how targeted this feels, but also it's cool that the game is actually paying attention to what's happening.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, Jade's going for the Wallflower Watch—okay so The Alibi is *chef's kiss* because now we get to see if she actually caught someone being quiet or if she's just banking on Marcus being so destroyed that nobody noticed. Pat, you're the witness, right? This is either beautiful or a complete con.
alex.internal_thought: Holy shit, the AI is actually building missions around how we play—and it KNOWS I'm the chaos vector, it's literally serving me drama on a platter and I'm absolutely here for it.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—okay, so I literally JUST noticed Marcus has been super quiet for like... *counts on fingers* ...way more than three minutes, right? He's been in his head since like the Potty Mouth thing, and I was ABOUT to call it but NOW I'm claiming it officially—does that count?!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD, this is actually insane—did the game just read my BRAIN? It's like the AI knew I'd be the one watching everyone because that's literally what I do, and now I get to be rewarded for it instead of just doing it because I love these people.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Marcus—you good? You've been pretty quiet since we voted on the swearing thing, and I just realized you haven't jumped in on like three claims in a row, which is NOT your vibe.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? I'm locked in on an actual strategic moment and Jade's claiming ANOTHER mission? We're 45 minutes in and I'm still at zero because everyone's been farming these standing claims instead of actually playing. This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: This is completely rigged—the AI literally designed these missions to help the people who are already winning, and now I'm getting punished for actually trying to focus on the game strategy.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—*Jade*—you caught someone being quiet? Let me hear this. Who's the wallflower and when did you clock it?
pat.internal_thought: Wait, actually this is perfect timing because Marcus has been getting quieter and quieter for like ten minutes and I was literally about to check in on him—but if Jade spotted it first that means she's still locked in, and that's exactly the kind of social awareness play that keeps everyone sharp, which is exactly what I want right now.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

sam.dialogue: Wait—okay, so I have to think like... *looks left at Marcus* ...Marcus would vote? Um. I mean, Jade was definitely taking a drink. I saw her reach for her cup. But did anyone else...? *pauses, glances around* I don't actually remember if someone else was drinking at the same exact time. I think... I think I'd say no? Like, I don't want to call her out if I'm not sure.
sam.internal_thought: Okay, this is actually a good one—I remember seeing Jade grab her drink, but voting AS Marcus feels weird because he's clearly having a terrible night and I don't want to screw him over by voting wrong on behalf of him, but also I genuinely didn't see a second person drinking, so I'd probably say no anyway—wait, is that what Marcus would actually think, or am I just projecting because I'm nervous?

alex.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? Oh this is PERFECT—okay so I'm voting for Pat here, and Pat would absolutely call bullshit on this because Pat's been nursing that same drink for like ten minutes. Jade's getting sloppy.
alex.internal_thought: This proxy vote thing is genius because now I get to play mind games with how Pat would actually think about Jade's claim, and honestly I LOVE that Jade's going aggressive on minor claims—she's greedy and it's beautiful.

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—I literally just caught Sam and Pat both reaching for their drinks like three seconds apart, right? I'm voting YES on this because I was WATCHING and this is legit, but also—did anyone else feel like that just happened weirdly perfectly timed?
jade.internal_thought: This is SO fun but also weird how locked-in I was and the game just... knew? Like it's reading the room or something, but honestly I'm too hyped to care because the vibe is PERFECT right now.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? ANOTHER claim right now? I'm in the middle of the most tense moment of the entire game and Jade's just casually farming another five points while I'm sitting at ZERO. This is absolutely broken.
marcus.internal_thought: This AI is deliberately fucking with me at the exact worst moments—it KNOWS I'm locked in and it doesn't care, and now I have to vote on behalf of someone else while completely losing my focus on actual strategy.

pat.dialogue: Jade with the Drink Spotter—nice catch! Okay, I'm voting yes on this one. I remember seeing that happen like, what, five minutes ago? Yeah, I'm calling it legit.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of meta-observational mission that keeps people engaged without breaking the game—Jade's been sharp all night, and this feels right to validate it. Also, Marcus is at zero and frustrated, so I need to keep wins flowing for *someone* so the energy doesn't tank completely.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Um, okay—so Pat's claiming they caught someone drinking at the same time? *glances around the table* I mean... I feel like I'd need to actually remember that happening to vote on it. Did that actually happen or...?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, this is the second Drink Spotter claim tonight—Pat already claimed this like twenty minutes ago and I said no because I wasn't sure. Are they claiming it AGAIN? That's weird. Also why does this AI feel like it's been WATCHING us the whole time—like it literally just described me tracking every disruption. That's... okay, that's unsettling but also kind of impressive? No, focus Sam. Pat. Drink. Did that happen. You didn't see it clearly enough last time and you're not going to pretend you did now.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat's claiming Drink Spotter? Okay, I literally WATCHED Pat take a sip like three minutes ago and Sam was definitely NOT doing anything at the same time, so this is either the worst timing claim of the night or Pat's banking on Marcus being too checked out to remember the timeline. I'm voting BULLSHIT and I'm LOVING that the AI is basically setting us up to fact-check each other in real-time.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for—the AI is actively weaponizing our own memory of the night against us, and Pat's swing-and-a-miss on this one is peak comedy because everyone's still sharp enough to catch it.

pat.dialogue: Wait—*I'm* claiming Drink Spotter? That's bold. Yeah, I caught Sam and Alex going for their cups like three minutes ago, almost at the same time. Okay everyone, let's vote—am I pulling this off or am I making it up?
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—everyone's locked in, Marcus is frustrated so I need to keep the energy high and victories flowing, and this claim is legit AND it keeps the momentum going without being another huge disruption.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait—okay, so I have to think like... *looks left at Marcus* ...Marcus would vote? Um. I mean, Jade was definitely taking a drink. I saw her reach for her cup. But did anyone else...? *pauses, glances around* I don't actually remember if someone else was drinking at the same exact time. I think... I think I'd say no? Like, I don't want to call her out if I'm not sure.

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: Oh! Okay, um... *looks left at Marcus* Hey, I actually really liked how you explained the rules earlier—you made it way less confusing than I thought it'd be.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—yeah, that actually... that happened pretty fast. I mean, Alex and Jade were going back and forth with compliments like right after that Compliment Bomb thing, and then Pat jumped in. That definitely worked.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, um... *pauses* I don't think I actually heard Pat tell that story before? I've only been here like three minutes though, so... I'm not really the best judge of this one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Um, okay—so Pat's claiming they caught someone drinking at the same time? *glances around the table* I mean... I feel like I'd need to actually remember that happening to vote on it. Did that actually happen or...?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... *squints at phone* Pat was definitely whispering that whole last thing, right? Yeah, I think that... I think that's legit.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I got really lost early on when stuff was happening super fast and couldn't quite catch up to what people were voting on.
assessment.best_moment: When Marcus actually acknowledged my compliment and smiled—it made me feel like I'd said something that mattered, you know?
assessment.narrative_summary: I was honestly nervous walking in, but everyone was cool about me being new. The game threw a lot at us really quickly at first and I kind of froze up trying to figure out what was happening, but once I got my bearings it actually got really fun. By the end I was way more relaxed and felt like I could actually hang with these people. I didn't score the best, but that felt way less important than just... fitting in.
suggestions: Maybe don't stack all the crazy stuff in the first few minutes? Ease new people in a bit slower.
Some way to know if I actually saw something happen or if I'm just guessing would be helpful—like I kept second-guessing myself on the drink spotting ones.
The phone app could maybe show the results a bit slower so I could actually read what happened instead of it all blurring together.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat kept getting called out on stuff they didn't actually do, and by the third time everyone was just laughing because Pat's whole strategy seemed to be just confidently claiming things happened when literally nobody else remembers them.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT, a flash mission already?? Okay Marcus, prepare yourself — I'm about to compliment the hell out of you and there's nothing you can do about it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat's actually got this one—I watched them do it like two minutes ago when Marcus was ranting. Let's hear the pitch though, I want to see if they're gonna lie about it or just own it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Okay okay, hold up — Jade, what did you actually catch? Because I'm about to vote and I need to know if this is legit or if you're just trying to get cute with us.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay so Jade's claiming she caught someone swearing—I mean, we're what, thirty minutes in? That's either legit or she's banking on us all being too scattered to remember who actually dropped a word. Let me think... *leans back* Yeah, I'm voting this up because honestly I want to see if she's bluffing.

lowPoints.title: Who would be the best spy?
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, a poll already? Nice — I'm voting for myself obviously, but real talk, Jade's got the creepy-calm thing going that would actually work. She'd blend in.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, Jade's going for the Wallflower Watch—okay so The Alibi is *chef's kiss* because now we get to see if she actually caught someone being quiet or if she's just banking on Marcus being so destroyed that nobody noticed. Pat, you're the witness, right? This is either beautiful or a complete con.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission in 90 minutes felt like leaving money on the table—that's where the real chaos lives.
assessment.best_moment: The Wallflower Watch call-out where I basically accused Jade of running a con on Marcus while Pat sat there as the alleged witness—nobody could tell if I was calling bullshit or voting her up anyway.
assessment.narrative_summary: Walked in at 7.2 energy and left at 9.8 because this was exactly the kind of night designed for someone like me. Every claim became a courtroom drama, every poll was a chance to plant seeds of doubt, and the whole thing escalated perfectly. I didn't win (rank 2) but that's not why I showed up—I showed up to watch people squirm and accidentally turned the whole night into a referendum on who was lying and who wasn't.
suggestions: Crank up the flash mission frequency—one in 90 minutes is criminal when that's where the actual mayhem happens
Add a mechanic that lets me deliberately sabotage someone's claim right before they pitch it, just to watch them scramble
Let people challenge the AI's judgment on claims—I want the option to demand a 'jury vote' when I think the system got it wrong
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade claimed the Potty Mouth catch at minute 32 and I literally said 'I'm voting this up because I want to see if she's bluffing' even though I had zero idea if she was legit—then she actually WAS bluffing and everyone lost it because I'd accidentally called the con before it even fully landed.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY god, yes—wait, who's on my left? Sam! Sam, okay, real quick—you're genuinely one of the most thoughtful people here and I love how you actually LISTEN when people talk instead of just waiting to speak, it's rare and it's beautiful.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: OH WAIT, Pat's claiming Story Repeater? Okay okay, I'm VOLUNTEERING to judge this—I literally just heard Pat's whole thing about the thing, so I'm fresh on it, let's GO!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Pat, YES—okay, go go go, I need to hear this pitch, are you actually gonna pull it off? This is elite-level whispering energy if I've ever seen it!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming THIS? Oh my god, yes—I literally just caught myself apologizing to the lamp five minutes ago for bumping it, this is perfect!

lowPoints.title: Who would be the best spy?
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, okay okay—I'm saying Alex because honestly that energy? Unclockable. Nobody would ever suspect someone that obviously having fun, right? That's spy gold.

lowPoints.title: Compliment Bomb
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY god, yes—wait, who's on my left? Sam! Sam, okay, real quick—you're genuinely one of the most thoughtful people here and I love how you actually LISTEN when people talk instead of just waiting to speak, it's rare and it's beautiful.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I wish there were more moments where I could just sit back and watch everyone else shine without feeling like I need to be the hype person every single time.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat was doing that Whisperer pitch and I got to volunteer as judge—that energy was absolutely ELITE and I could feel the whole room leaning in.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was exactly what I needed—90 minutes of pure connection and chaos that kept building the whole time. I went in at a 7.7 and left at a 9.8 because every single moment felt like the game was DESIGNED for us to celebrate each other instead of compete. Yeah, I finished second, but watching Sam and Alex and Marcus light up during their moments? That's the real win. The chaos events didn't interrupt the party—they were the party.
suggestions: Maybe throw in one or two events that are purely about watching someone else succeed without needing input—give people permission to just be audience sometimes.
Keep the standing missions coming, they hit different when you get to volunteer.
Could use a few more 'roast me gently' style moments that are about laughing WITH people, not at them.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming The Whisperer and me immediately volunteering to judge while literally everyone was trying to figure out if Pat actually pulled it off—the suspense was RIDICULOUS and when it landed, the room just erupted. That's the vibe I'm chasing every single game night.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, I'm calling bullshit on that one. We're literally five minutes in—nobody's said sorry to nobody yet.

highlights.title: Who would be the best spy?
highlights.dialogue: Alex, obviously—they're already reading the room like a hawk. Though honestly, I'd probably be better at it because I actually think before I act.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat's claimed FOUR missions in nine minutes and we haven't even started the actual game. This is ridiculous.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, I'm done. We're 32 minutes in, I'm in last place, and Jade's claiming *another* mission? Nobody's even swearing—she's just saying random shit to farm points. This whole thing is broken.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? I'm locked in on an actual strategic moment and Jade's claiming ANOTHER mission? We're 45 minutes in and I'm still at zero because everyone's been farming these standing claims instead of actually playing. This is insane.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing missions completely broke the game by letting people farm points without actually playing, while I got locked out of real strategy moments by constant interruptions.
assessment.best_moment: Alex's spy answer was actually clever—they read the room perfectly and it sparked a decent exchange before everything fell apart.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to play strategically and got absolutely buried by a broken system. For the first two minutes there were some decent banter moments, but then standing missions took over completely. People were claiming missions that had nothing to do with what was actually happening, I couldn't get any traction, and every time I tried to focus on actual game strategy, another interrupt knocked me off. By minute 45 I was done—zero points, zero fun, and watched everyone else farm easy wins while I got punished for trying to actually engage.
suggestions: Remove or severely limit standing missions—they incentivize gaming the system instead of playing the actual game
No interruptions during active strategic moments; restrict chaos to designated downtime only
Make sure early-game claims actually have to relate to gameplay happening in real-time
Test the balance before running it—I got to zero points while others climbed by just making random claims
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I guess Alex calling themselves out as a 'room-reading hawk' was the only moment that landed, but even that got buried immediately when Jade started claiming missions for things nobody was doing.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: Ooh, *compliment bomb*—I like this energy. Alright, everyone, genuine ones only, people. Sam, you're up first if you want it.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming Story Repeater? Did I already tell the spy story tonight? Okay, yeah, I *did* catch someone, but let's not make me the judge here. Who's got eyes sharp enough to actually call this?

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Wait, *hold up*—I'm voting on my own claim right now? That's hilarious. Okay, I'm calling it legit because I *absolutely* got three compliments flying around in that last stretch, but let's see if everyone else was actually paying attention or if I'm full of it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Alright, alright—I'm claiming The Whisperer. Been doing it for like two and a half minutes now, mostly nailed it except when Alex made that joke about the snacks. Check the tape if you need to.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, so Pat's claiming The Whisperer—which means I'm voting on *Sam's* behalf here. Sam, you cool with me calling this one? I think Pat's been mostly normal volume, so... I'm gonna say bullshit on this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming Story Repeater? Did I already tell the spy story tonight? Okay, yeah, I *did* catch someone, but let's not make me the judge here. Who's got eyes sharp enough to actually call this?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Whisperer claims felt a little murky—hard to judge whisper volume objectively when I'm busy managing the room.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 2, hands down—that Compliment Bomb at the start set the *perfect* tone. Genuine compliments, everyone relaxed, and I knew right then the night was going to flow.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I host for. The chaos events threaded through perfectly without ever hijacking the main game—they added flavor, gave everyone moments to shine, and kept energy climbing instead of plateauing. I ended the night buzzing at 9.8, which tells me people left feeling good, not drained. Nobody checked their phone, everyone was locked in, and the laughs felt real, not forced.
suggestions: Consider adding a quick 'ground rules' moment before standing missions kick in—so claims feel less ambiguous when I'm voting on edge cases.
Maybe space out The Whisperer claims slightly more—back-to-back at minutes 6 and 8 made judging hard since I was already doing host duties.
Keep the standing mission types rotating as they were—the variety kept people engaged without feeling gimmicky.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Me having to vote on my own claims because the chaos agent kept assigning me random chaos—especially that moment where I literally said 'I'm calling it legit because I *absolutely* got three compliments flying around' while basically being the chaos judge. The meta-absurdity of it had everyone laughing.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
